ShaderNodePreset.Description:
ColorExpression - a Matchbox re-creation of Nuke's Color > Math > Expression node.
The per-channel formulas live in the EXPRESSION BLOCK inside ColorExpression.glsl and use Nuke-identical variables (r g b a, x y, cx cy, width height, frame) and the full Nuke function library (sin, pow, clamp, lerp, noise, fBm, from_sRGB ...).
Edit the four channel lines, then recompile with: shader_builder -m ColorExpression.glsl
The knobs below (k1..k4, Reference Colour) are animatable and can be referenced from the expressions without recompiling. See README.md for the full parity reference.
ShaderNodePreset.Name: ColorExpression

Uniform.Tooltip: Image input (the rgba layer in Nuke).
Uniform.Name: front

Uniform.Tooltip: Generic scalar. Refer to it as k1 in your expressions.
Uniform.Name: k1

Uniform.Tooltip: Generic scalar. Refer to it as k2 in your expressions.
Uniform.Name: k2

Uniform.Tooltip: Generic scalar. Refer to it as k3 in your expressions.
Uniform.Name: k3

Uniform.Tooltip: Generic scalar. Refer to it as k4 in your expressions.
Uniform.Name: k4

Uniform.Tooltip: Reference colour. Refer to it as ref.r ref.g ref.b in your expressions.
Uniform.Name: ref

Uniform.Tooltip: Blend between the original image (0) and the expression result (1).
Uniform.Name: mix_amount

Uniform.Tooltip: Clamp the result to the 0..1 range (like Nuke's clamp(x)).
Uniform.Name: clamp_output
